description: CSV injection risk: csv.reader reads user CSV into _raw_data, data may be exported to Excel via to_excel(). Malicious formulas like =cmd may trigger when user opens Excel.
code_snippet: with SafeOpen(file) as csv_file: csv_content = csv.reader(csv_file) for row in csv_content: self._raw_data.append(row)
data_flow:
[SOURCE] profiling_parser.py:174 SafeOpen(file) -> 176 csv.reader -> 178 _raw_data
[PROPAGATION] op_summary.py:94 -> op_list
[SINK OUT] npu_slow_advice.py:42 to_excel()

description: check_path_valid lacks complete PathManager validation. Only checks existence, filetype, symlink and size, missing path traversal prevention for dot-dot sequences.
code_snippet: def check_path_valid(path): if not os.path.exists(path): raise FileNotFoundError() if os.path.islink(path): raise FileNotFoundError()
data_flow:
[SOURCE] timeline_event_dataset.py:176 open(self.timeline_file)
[MISSING VALIDATION] check_path_valid missing PathManager.input_path_common_check
[SINK] open() reads user path

description: SafeOpen class lacks PathManager validation. Only checks file existence and readability, does not prevent path traversal attacks via dot-dot sequences.
code_snippet: class SafeOpen: def __init__(self, name, mode='r'): if os.access(name, os.R_OK): self.file = open(name, mode)
data_flow:
[SOURCE] profiling_parser.py:174 SafeOpen(file)
[MISSING VALIDATION] SafeOpen missing PathManager.check_input_file_path
[SINK] open(name, mode) opens user path

description: PathManager regex does not block path traversal. Regex allows single dot matching, consecutive dots (..) pass validation. Symlink check and owner check provide partial mitigation.
code_snippet: def input_path_common_check(cls, path): if os.path.islink(path): raise RuntimeError() pattern = ... if not re.fullmatch(pattern, path): raise RuntimeError()
data_flow:
[SOURCE] user path parameter
[VALIDATION GAP] regex allows dot-dot sequences
[PROPAGATION] advice_factory.py:42 -> collection_path
[SINK OUT] PathManager.limited_depth_walk()

description: Database content parsed with json.loads. Database file from user profiling dir may be tampered. json.loads itself does not execute code but parsed data may flow to dangerous operations.
code_snippet: df['args'] = df['args'].apply(lambda x: json.loads(x) if pd.notna(x) else {})
data_flow:
[SOURCE] _execute_sql(db_path) -> db query result
[SINK] json.loads(x) -> JSON parse
[SINK OUT] TaskInfo(record) -> timeline processing
